Tests the property descriptors of `Number.prototype` for writability, enumerability, and configurability.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that checks the property descriptor of the `prototype` property on the `Number` object in JavaScript. It uses `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor` to retrieve the descriptor and assigns it to the variable `a`. The script then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the `writable`, `enumerable`, and `configurable` attributes of the `prototype` property are all set to `false`. This ensures that the `prototype` property of `Number` is non-writable, non-enumerable, and non-configurable, which is consistent with the ECMAScript specification.
